sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_timestamps (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, ts1 TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, ts2 TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);
INSERT INTO test_timestamps (id, ts1, ts2) VALUES (1, timestamp '2021-12-31 18:40:40+00', timestamp '2021-12-20 16:30:20+00');
INSERT INTO test_timestamps (id, ts1, ts2) VALUES (2, NULL, timestamp '2023-01-01 00:00:00+00');
INSERT INTO test_timestamps (id, ts1, ts2) VALUES (3, timestamp '2010-12-31 18:40:40+00', timestamp '2010-01-01 00:00:00+00');
SELECT id, timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) + (91 & 15) AS result FROM test_timestamps WHERE ts1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, timestampdiff_day(ts1, COALESCE(ts2, timestamp '1970-01-01 00:00:00+00')) + (91 & 7) AS new_value FROM test_timestamps WHERE ts1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_timestamps WHERE ts1 IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count, timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) AS date_difference FROM test_timestamps HAVING date_difference > 10;

SELECT id, timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) AS difference, ABS(difference & -1) AS abs_value FROM (SELECT id, timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) AS difference FROM test_timestamps) AS derived HAVING abs_value = 0;
SELECT DISTINCT id, (timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) & (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_timestamps)) AS bitwise FROM test_timestamps WHERE (timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE further_tests (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, timestamp_diff INTEGER, AND_value INTEGER);
INSERT INTO further_tests (timestamp_diff, AND_value) SELECT timestampdiff_day(ts1, ts2) + (91 & 15), (91 & 12) FROM test_timestamps;
SELECT * FROM further_tests WHERE timestamp_diff > 0 AND AND_value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY timestamp_diff DESC;
